title: Youth Residential Support Worker
description:
Are you looking for a flexible role where you can make a real impact on the lives of young people in care?

Purpose of the Job

As a Children's mainstream Residential Support Worker, you'll provide essential support to young people aged 12-17 living in mainstream residential homes. Your role is to offer stability, promote independence, and create a safe, nurturing environment where young people can thrive.

This is ad-hoc, temporary work, ideal for those seeking flexibility while still wanting to make a meaningful difference.

Key Details of the Job

- Location: various locations in Leeds
- Pay: £14.82 per hour + uplifts for waking nights and weekends
- Contract: Ad-hoc temporary work (no guaranteed hours)
- Shifts: Earlies, lates, sleep-ins, waking nights

Requirements

- Ligature Training (mandatory)
- Minimum 1 year experience in residential childcare, youth support, safeguarding, or a similar environment
- Strong understanding of safeguarding, risk management, and de-escalation
- Experience engaging positively with young people who may display challenging behaviours

Desirable

- Experience in Mental Health Secure settings
- Additional behavioural management or risk-reduction training

Apply Today

If you're ready to support young people and work flexibly around your lifestyle, we'd love to hear from you.
summary: Flexible temporary residential support role for young people in care across Leeds. Includes varied shifts, safeguarding-focused work, and uplifts for nights and weekends.

title: Support Worker
description:
Are you good with people? Would you like to use your skills to support individuals to get into meaningful employment? We have a unique role working as a Support Worker in our Courtyard Apartments.

At Courtyard Services Horsforth, Leeds you will support people with learning disabilities, encouraging and empowering them to develop skills and confidence in a warm and vibrant setting.

The Role:

- You will work primarily in the Courtyard Apartments.
- You will work alongside support users to experience employment and community involvement.
- To prepare healthy, high quality food/drinks and maintain food hygiene and health and safety.
- To work alongside the wider team and offer practical and emotional support to members as required.
- To ensure the efficient general running of the service.

Part time hours available to be worked flexibly Monday to Saturday between 08:30-16:30, part time hours subject to a minimum of 22.5 hours per week.

Benefits of working with Creative Support:

We offer all employees competitive pay and a pension contribution in addition to 28 days annual leave. As an employer that is committed to the professional development of our staff we also support our care staff to complete the nationally recognised Care Certificate and pay for your enhanced DBS. Additional benefits include enhanced maternity leave, employee support programmes and an additional day of leave each year on or around your Birthday.

Vacancy Reference Number: 91288

Applications for this role must be submitted via the Creative Support website using the above vacancy reference number.

Creative Support's Development Pathway offers a structured induction and values-led training to help staff grow from entry-level to leadership roles. It empowers career progression through tailored learning aligned with national strategies and the WE CARE framework. Benefits of working with Creative Support include a probationary bonus, pension contributions, 28 days Leave and company paid enhanced DBS.

We are a passionate, inclusive, and anti-racist organization - Stonewall Diversity Champion, Disability Confident Employer who have recently received Investors in People Gold award.

We can only accept applications from candidates who are located in and eligible to work within the UK.
summary: Leeds support worker role focused on helping people with learning disabilities build confidence, employment skills, and community involvement. Part-time hours with training and benefits.

title: Support Worker Driver
description:
Creative Support are looking for warm, reliable and proactive Support Workers to join our team in Bradford to provide quality care and support to individuals with mental health needs.

Your role will include:

- Developing warm and trusting relationships with service users, encouraging them to express their needs, views and concerns
- Working in partnership with service users through the recovery process, being flexible and responsive to the individual needs of the service users
- Helping service users to develop recovery focused support goals, promoting hope and aspiration
- Respecting the service users' right to privacy, ensuring that their dignity is maintained at all times

Creative Support's Development Pathway offers a structured induction and values-led training to help staff grow from entry-level to leadership roles. It empowers career progression through tailored learning aligned with national strategies and the WE CARE framework. Benefits of working with Creative Support include a probationary bonus, pension contributions, 28 days Leave and company paid enhanced DBS.

You will be required to work in a flexible manner including evenings, weekends and sleep-ins. You must have a full, clean, valid UK driving licence and access to a car for work.

We are a passionate, inclusive, and anti-racist organization. We are a Stonewall Diversity Champion, Disability Confident Employer who have recently received Investors in People Gold award. We actively encourage applications from candidates from all backgrounds and cultures.

As a Disability Confident Employer we welcome applications from people with a disability, and as a proud Stonewall Diversity Champion we are committed to ensuring our recruitment process is inclusive and accessible.
summary: Bradford support worker role supporting adults with mental health needs through recovery-focused care. Driving licence and car required, with flexible shifts including evenings and weekends.

title: Recovery Support Worker
description:
Are you looking for a rewarding career that is stimulating and varied each day?

Creative Support are looking for warm, reliable and proactive Support Workers to join our Floating Staff team in Bradford to provide quality care and support to individuals with mental health needs. We work across the city in four local teams, ensuring staff offer personalised mental health recovery support, alongside housing related support.

This is a temporary role to cover for maternity leave, expected to be approximately a year.

Your role will include:

- Developing warm and trusting relationships with service users, encouraging them to express their needs, views and concerns
- Working in partnership with service users through the recovery process, being flexible and responsive to the individual needs of the service users
- Assisting in the delivery of interventions to meet identified needs and goals
- Supporting service users in a manner which promotes dignity, wellbeing and independence
- Working positively with other agencies, parents, carers and other services to ensure person-centred support

Creative Support's Development Pathway offers a structured induction and values-led training to help staff grow from entry-level to leadership roles. It empowers career progression through tailored learning aligned with national strategies and the WE CARE framework. Benefits of working with Creative Support include a probationary bonus, pension contributions, 28 days Leave and company paid enhanced DBS.

This role will involve evening and weekend work, previous experience is essential. You will be based at one service and expected to cover at other local services as needed.

We are a passionate, inclusive, and anti-racist organization - Stonewall Diversity Champion, Disability Confident Employer and Investors in People Gold awarded.

You will be based in the Bradford city centre area within walking distance of all public transport routes and local amenities.
summary: Temporary Bradford recovery support role covering maternity leave for around a year. Focuses on mental health support, personalised recovery plans, and flexible citywide cover.

title: Foster Carer Support Worker
company: The Children's Family Trust • Company • Part Time
description:
THE CHILDREN'S FAMILY TRUST (THE CFT)

Is a well-established Ofsted Outstanding Registered Charity and an Independent Fostering Agency providing a wide range of foster placements. We firmly believe our Foster Carers are our most valuable resource deserving the highest levels of support and remuneration we can offer. We work in partnership with Local Authority Social Workers and are committed to keeping our children safe and secure in stable and loving families.

We are now recruiting for Foster Carer Support Buddies to work with our Foster Carers and their families. This is a great opportunity for those studying within a social work or health related field who want to gain experience around Child Protection, Child Development and safeguarding children.

This work is offered on a casual, hourly paid basis and consists of support work with Foster Carers and their family, specifically around the practical support of activities, household chores, homework, and social and life skills support.

Other responsibilities to include:

- Working alongside a Foster Carer, taking guidance and instructions from them regarding tasks and support requirements to help them in providing good levels of care to a looked-after child.
- Working individually to provide support to the Foster Carers' own children.
- Appropriate social activities e.g. bowling, cinema, shopping, arts and crafts and baking.
- Logging support and maintaining records.
- Being able to provide support around difficult and challenging behaviour.
- To undertake mandatory training to ensure adherence to CF safeguarding policy and compliance with safer caring.

Support is mostly required on weekday evenings, weekend and school holiday times.

Requirements

- Support or care experience, either personally or professionally.
- Relevant study or work within social care, health, childcare or safeguarding.
- Ability to build positive, boundaried relationships with children and families.
- Good record keeping and communication skills.
summary: Casual Wakefield support role helping foster carers and families with practical tasks, activities, and child-focused support. Well suited to applicants building experience in social care or safeguarding.

title: Support Worker
description:
We are looking for compassionate Support Workers to empower adults with learning disabilities to lead independent and fulfilling lives in Wakefield. We believe in recognizing and nurturing our clients' strengths, abilities, and personal resources.

Join us in providing personalised care and support, tailoring your approach to meet each individual's unique needs. We need warm, motivated and person-centred individuals who are passionate about making a positive impact. You'll be working in line with Creative Support's principles, promoting active and independent lifestyles for the people you support. Excellent verbal and written communication skills are essential, along with the ability to provide respectful personal care and support with domestic tasks, while adhering to health and safety guidelines.

Previous experience is essential. As a Support Worker, you'll engage in diverse tasks such as:

- Promoting active and independent lifestyles
- Supporting with personal care and domestic tasks
- Collaborating with families and professionals to deliver coordinated support
- Encouraging choice, dignity, and inclusion in everyday life

Creative Support's Development Pathway offers a structured induction and values-led training to help staff grow from entry-level to leadership roles. It empowers career progression through tailored learning aligned with national strategies and the WE CARE framework. Benefits of working with Creative Support include a probationary bonus, pension contributions, 28 days Leave and company paid enhanced DBS.

This role includes evening, weekend, and sleep-in shifts.
summary: Wakefield support worker role helping adults with learning disabilities live more independently. Includes personal care, domestic support, and flexible shifts.
